## Discount Factor

The discount factor $\gamma = 0.99$ is used throughout this thesis. The role
of $\gamma$ in the value function is defined in Chapter 4; this section
justifies the specific value chosen.

With $\gamma = 0.99$ the effective planning horizon is approximately
$1/(1-\gamma) = 100$ steps. On the HFT data used here — where each step
corresponds to a short intraday interval — this horizon covers a meaningful
window of market activity without extending so far that value estimates
accumulate excessive variance from stochastic rewards.

$\gamma = 0.99$ is held constant across all experiments to isolate the effect
of other design choices (state representation, reward function). Sensitivity
to $\gamma$ is a natural direction for future work, particularly whether lower
values (0.9–0.95) improve performance in choppy, mean-reverting regimes.
